text: ER TRIAGE NOTE: Patient ID: 882910. Date: 01/02/2024. Time: 02:14. Chief Complaint: Shortness of breath and fever. Patient presents with a 3-day history of increasing productive cough with yellow sputum, malaise, and fever peaking at 102.4F at home. PMH: Recent laparoscopic cholecystectomy (10 days ago). Vitals: T 103.1F, HR 118, BP 98/62, RR 26, O2 Sat 88% on RA. Physical Exam: Patient is in acute respiratory distress, accessory muscle use noted. Bilateral crackles at lung bases. CV: Tachycardic, regular rhythm. Abdomen: S/p cholecystectomy, sutures intact, no erythema. Assessment: Post-operative pneumonia vs. aspiration. Sepsis protocol initiated. Plan: Admit to ICU. Start Vancomycin and Piperacillin/Tazobactam. Bolus 2L NS. ICU TRANSFER NOTE: Date: 01/05/2024. Patient admitted via ED for septic shock secondary to pneumonia. Patient underwent laparoscopy on 12/28/23. Note: The patient was admitted to the ICU on Jan 5th. Course: Patient remained febrile (101.8F) on Day 2 of ICU stay. Sputum cultures grew S. pneumoniae, sensitive to ceftriaxone. Vasopressors (Norepinephrine) titrated off on 01/06. Hemodynamics stable. Renal function stable. Respiratory status improved; transitioned to 2L NC. Weaning of oxygen therapy successful. Plan: Complete 7-day course of antibiotics. D/c foley, continue physical therapy. STEP-DOWN UNIT NOTE: Date: 01/09/2024. Patient remains on 2L NC. Pulmonary status: Reduced crackles, improved air entry. Hemodynamics stable on room air. Lab values: WBC 10.4, Hgb 11.2, Cr 0.9. Medication Reconciliation Note: Vancomycin and Zosyn were discontinued at 0800 this morning. No reasoning documented for discontinuation prior to completion of the 7-day course. Patient tolerating oral diet. Ambulation improved with assist of one. PT continues daily. Plan: Transition to home environment. DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS: Patient Name: John Doe. Date of Discharge: 01/12/2024. Diagnosis: Resolved pneumonia s/p cholecystectomy. Discharge Medications: Lisinopril 10mg daily, Atorvastatin 20mg daily. Follow-up: Schedule appointment with PCP in 2 weeks. WARNING: Seek emergency care if you experience high fever (>101F), inability to breathe, or chest pain. Please be aware that your antibiotic therapy was concluded in the hospital. If you develop recurrence of symptoms, please report to the nearest urgent care center. Condition at Discharge: [BLANK]. Note: We acknowledge the confusion regarding the admission date discrepancies recorded in the electronic health record; the formal chart audit will be conducted next month to address documentation errors. Please maintain strict hydration and rest for the next 7 days. Your surgical site from the previous cholecystectomy appears to be healing appropriately despite the respiratory complication. If you have questions regarding the cessation of your intravenous medications or the timeline of your admission, please contact the hospital patient advocacy department.
